message:
franken_networkx: harden GML node/edge validation and named entity unescape

Tighten the EdgeListEngine::read_gml parser against malformed or hostile
GML inputs, and complete the named-entity decoder used by gml_unescape.

Node validation:
- Track a label_set alongside id_to_label so duplicate node ids and
  duplicate labels are both detected in a single pass.
- In Strict compatibility mode, duplicate ids, missing labels, and
  duplicate labels now FailClosed via record() with confidence 1.0.
- In Lenient mode the same conditions append a warning and record a
  FullValidate decision (confidence 0.6 for the missing-label case,
  0.7 for the duplicate cases), then skip the offending node so the
  rest of the graph still loads.
- Nodes with no explicit label fall back to the numeric id as before,
  but only after recording the warning.

Edge validation:
- Edges that reference an unknown source or target id are now surfaced.
  Strict mode FailCloses; lenient mode warns and, if the synthetic
  string-form id is not already taken, inserts a placeholder node so
  the edge can still be added. If the synthetic label collides with an
  existing label, the edge is skipped entirely instead of silently
  corrupting the graph.

gml_unescape:
- Resolve the five standard XML named entities (& " < >
  ') that the previous implementation dropped on the floor. Numeric
  entities (&#NN;) continue to work unchanged.

No behavior change for well-formed GML inputs; only adds diagnostics and
fail-closed handling for malformed ones.
